questions.question: What is the topic for Week 16?
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly listed for Lesson Sixteen is DESTINY DESTROYERS.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 15?
questions.explanation: Untoward Freedom was the topic for Week 15.

questions.question: For I know the thoughts that I think toward you, says the LORD, thoughts of ________ and not of evil, to give you a future and a hope.
questions.explanation: The memory verse (Jeremiah 29:11) says 'thoughts of peace and not of evil'.

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is Jeremiah 29:11.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 16 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: For I know the thoughts that I think toward you, says the LORD, thoughts of peace and not of evil, to give you a future and a hope.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of Jeremiah 29:11.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (Jeremiah 29:11), what two things does God intend to give you?
options: C. A future and a hope
questions.explanation: The verse says 'to give you a future and a hope.'

questions.question: How is 'destiny' defined in the introduction of the Week 16 lesson?
options: A. A random series of events
B. God's divine purpose and preplanned destination for a person's life
D. Whatever happens at the end of your life
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Destiny is God’s divine purpose and preplanned destination for a person’s life.'

questions.question: Which verse is cited to show that destiny represents what God has ordained for an individual even before conception?
questions.explanation: The text links this to '(Jeremiah 1:5)'.

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'EXAMPLES OF DESTINY DESTROYERS'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, what leads to destruction due to lack of focus and is described as a significant destiny destroyer? (Hosea 4:6)
questions.explanation: Point 1 is 'Ignorance: Lack of knowledge of God’s plan... (Hosea 4:6).'

questions.question: According to the Week 16 lesson, Solomon's pursuit of vanity and wealth led to his backsliding.
questions.explanation: Point 2 states 'Solomon’s pursuit of vanity and wealth led to his backsliding and loss of direction.'

questions.question: Which destiny destroyer leads to divine resistance? (James 4:6)
questions.explanation: Point 4 says 'Pride leads to divine resistance (James 4:6).'

questions.question: Which scriptures are associated with 'Lack of Faith' as a destiny destroyer?
questions.explanation: Point 3 links this to '(1 Timothy 1:19; Hebrews 11:6)'.

questions.question: ________ creates openings for the enemy to attack one's destiny, because maintaining alignment with God's purpose requires it (Matthew 13:25).
questions.explanation: Point 8 is 'Prayerlessness: This creates openings for the enemy...'

questions.question: Living in unconfessed sin only hinders earthly purpose, but does not affect eternal promise.
questions.explanation: Point 5 says 'Living in sin hinders both earthly purpose and eternal promise (1 John 3:4).'

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'BIBLICAL INSTANCES'.

questions.question: Who allowed greed and betrayal to destroy his destiny according to Outline II?
questions.explanation: Point 1 lists 'Judas Iscariot: He allowed greed and betrayal to destroy his destiny...'

questions.question: Which companion of apostle Paul abandoned his faith and ministry, choosing worldly desires over his divine calling?
questions.explanation: Point 2 lists 'Demas: Once a companion of the apostle Paul... choosing worldly desires...'

questions.question: Who was the greedy servant of Elisha whose actions resulted in the curse of leprosy upon him and his descendants?
questions.explanation: Point 3 lists 'Gehazi: The greedy servant of Elisha... resulted in the curse of leprosy...'

questions.question: Why did Reuben lose his birthright, according to the Week 16 lesson?
options: A. Due to disobedience in taking spoils from Jericho
B. Due to greed and betrayal
C. Due to illicit sex with his father's concubine
D. Due to pride and backsliding
questions.explanation: Point 4 lists 'Reuben: Jacob’s firstborn who lost his birthright due to illicit sex with his father’s concubine.'

questions.question: Whose disobedience in taking spoils from Jericho led to the destruction of his family and the defeat of Israel in battle?
questions.explanation: Point 5 lists 'Achan: His disobedience in taking spoils from Jericho led to the destruction of his family...'

questions.question: Which scripture verse is associated with Gehazi's story in the Week 16 lesson?
questions.explanation: The text cites '(2 Kings 5:20-27)' for Gehazi.

questions.question: According to the conclusion, to avoid destiny destroyers we must fully surrender to God and align ourselves with His will.
questions.explanation: The conclusion states exactly this: 'we must fully surrender to Him and align ourselves with His will.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'But King Solomon loved many foreign women, as well as the daughter of Pharaoh...'
questions.verse_text: But King Solomon loved many foreign women, as well as the daughter of Pharaoh...
questions.explanation: This is verse 1 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (1 Kings 11:1), which of these groups of women is NOT listed as those Solomon loved?
questions.explanation: The text lists 'Moabites, Ammonites, Edomites, Sidonians, and Hittites'.

questions.question: Why did the Lord tell the children of Israel not to intermarry with these nations? (1 Kings 11:2)
options: A. They would steal their wealth
B. They will turn away your hearts after their gods
C. They were enemies of peace
questions.explanation: Verse 2 says 'Surely they will turn away your hearts after their gods.'

questions.question: According to 1 Kings 11:3, how many wives (princesses) and concubines did Solomon have?
questions.explanation: Verse 3 states 'he had seven hundred wives, princesses, and three hundred concubines'.

questions.question: What happened when Solomon was old? (1 Kings 11:4)
options: A. He repented of his sins
B. He wrote the book of Psalms
C. His wives turned his heart after other gods
D. He lost all his wealth
questions.explanation: Verse 4 says 'when Solomon was old, that his wives turned his heart after other gods'.

questions.question: According to 1 Kings 11:5, which goddess of the Sidonians did Solomon go after?
questions.explanation: Verse 5 says 'Solomon went after Ashtoreth the goddess of the Sidonians'.

questions.question: According to 1 Kings 11:5, what is Milcom referred to as?
options: A. The god of the Edomites
B. The abomination of the Ammonites
C. The golden calf of Israel
questions.explanation: Verse 5 says 'and after Milcom the abomination of the Ammonites.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show that uncontrolled anger can cause individuals to deviate from God's purpose?
questions.explanation: Point 6 lists '(Proverbs 29:22)' for Anger.

questions.question: Laziness prevents individuals from fulfilling their potential, leaving them perpetually under the influence of more industrious individuals.
questions.explanation: Point 7 explicitly states this.

questions.question: While destiny is a beautiful and purposeful plan, it is still influenced by one's attitude and willingness to ________ with God's will.
questions.explanation: The text says 'influenced by one’s attitude and willingness to align with God’s will.'

questions.question: Which verse is associated with Reuben losing his birthright in the Week 16 lesson?
questions.explanation: Point 4 cites '(Genesis 49:3-4)' for Reuben.

questions.question: The Bible Passage (1 Kings 11:6) states that Solomon did evil in the sight of the Lord and did not fully follow the Lord, unlike his father David.
questions.explanation: Verse 6 states: 'Solomon did evil in the sight of the Lord, and did not fully follow the Lord, as did his father David.'
